settings.title: Ten Years, Forty Tonnes of Compost
settings.excerpt: A decade in, we did the sums on what we have planted, and the number that stuck with us was the soil.
settings.body:
This month marks ten years since the first Greenfield job, a tired front garden three streets from where the yard stands now.

We pulled the records for fun. In ten years we have planted just over two thousand trees and shrubs and spread roughly forty tonnes of compost we make from our own green waste.

To mark it, we are donating a full planting day to the community garden by the canal, which has kept volunteers busy for years on a shoestring. New beds, fresh soil, and a small orchard going in at the back.

Thank you to every client who let us dig up their garden and trusted us to put it back better. Here is to the next ten.
